Lecture Transcription

Start Lecture Transcribing.

TEXT 15

“īhate bhagavān īśo

na hi tatra visajjate

ātma-lābhena pūrṇārtho

nāvasīdanti ye 'nu tam”

Srimad Bhagavatam 8.1.15

SYNONYMS

īhate—engages in activities of creation, maintenance and annihilation; bhagavān—the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Kṛṣṇa; īśaḥ—the supreme controller; na—not; hi—indeed; tatra—in such activities; visajjate—He becomes entangled; ātma-lābhena—because of His own gain; pūrṇa-arthaḥ—who is self-satisfied; na—not; avasīdanti—are disheartened; ye—persons who; anu—follow; tam—the Supreme Personality of Godhead.

TRANSLATION

The Supreme Personality of Godhead is full in opulence by His own gain, yet He acts as the creator, maintainer and annihilator of this material world. In spite of acting in that way, He is never entangled. Hence devotees who follow in His footsteps are also never entangled.

PURPORT

As stated in Bhagavad-gītā (3.9), yajñārthāt karmaṇo 'nyatra loko 'yaṁ karma-bandhanaḥ: "Work done as a sacrifice for Viṣṇu has to be performed, otherwise work binds one to this material world." If we do not act in Kṛṣṇa consciousness we shall be entangled, like silkworms in cocoons. The Supreme Personality of Godhead, Kṛṣṇa, appears in order to teach us how to work so that we will not be entangled in this material world. Our real problem is that we are entangled in materialistic activities, and because we are conditioned, our struggle continues through punishment in material existence in one body after another in different forms of life. As the Lord says in Bhagavad-gītā (15.7):

“mamaivāṁśo jīva-loke

jīva-bhūtaḥ sanātanaḥ

manaḥ ṣaṣṭhānīndriyāṇi

prakṛti-sthāni karṣati”

"The living entities in this conditioned world are My eternal, fragmental parts. Due to conditioned life, they are struggling very hard with the six senses, which include the mind." The living entities are actually minute forms who are part and parcel of the Supreme Lord. The Supreme Lord is full in everything and the small particles of the Lord are also originally qualified like Him, but because of their minute existence, they are infected by material attraction and thus entangled. We must therefore follow the instructions of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, and then, like Kṛṣṇa, who is never entangled by His material activities of creation, maintenance and annihilation, we will have nothing for which to lament (nāvasīdanti ye 'nu tam). Kṛṣṇa personally gives instructions in Bhagavad-gītā, and anyone who follows these instructions is liberated.

Following Kṛṣṇa's instructions is possible when one is a devotee, for Kṛṣṇa instructs that one should become a devotee. Man-manā bhava mad-bhakto mad-yājī māṁ namaskuru: "Always think of Me and become My devotee. Worship Me and offer your homage unto Me." (Bg. 18.65) Always thinking of Kṛṣṇa means chanting the Hare Kṛṣṇa mantra, but unless one is an initiated devotee, he cannot do this. As soon as one becomes a devotee, he engages in Deity worship (mad-yājī). A devotee's business is to offer obeisances to the Lord and the spiritual master constantly. This principle is the recognized way to come to the platform of bhakti. As soon as one comes to this platform, he gradually understands the Supreme Personality of Godhead, and simply by understanding Kṛṣṇa one is liberated from material bondage.

Start Lecture by His Holiness Radhanath Swami Maharaja.

“karmaṇy evādhikāras te

mā phaleṣu kadācana

mā karma-phala-hetur bhūr

mā te saṅgo 'stv akarmaṇi”

Bhagavad-Gita 2.47

Bhagavad-Gita teaches us that we have the right to perform our duty, but we are never entitled to the fruits of our activities. At the same time, we must know that we are not the cause of the results of our activities and we can never be attached to not doing our duty. So, our duty is the instruction of the spiritual master and Krishna. We have the right to perform our duty. In fact, it is the greatest privilege, the greatest benediction to have the right to engage in devotional service. Whatever that service may be? There is no greater gain that can be attained within this entire world than simply the execution of devotional service. Material vision discriminates between one service and another. But one who understands the transcendental nature of devotional service, he sees that anything that is done under the order of guru for the pleasure of Krishna they all are on the transcendental platform.

If your spiritual master tells you to clean the toilets or the spiritual master ask you to give a lecture to hundred and thousand people there is no difference, they are both completely on the transcendental platform. They are both by the power of bhakti the means of deliverance. In fact, if the spiritual master sincerely wants you to clean the toilet and you give the lecture to hundred thousand people instead cleaning the toilet would have been a higher service. Because our spiritual advancement comes through the process of pleasing the spiritual master. Then of course in a very practical situation it comes through pleasing those who are representing the spiritual master, who are his entrusted servants and servants of those servants, who are giving order on his behalf. But it is through the process of pleasing guru that we are engaged in bhakti Yoga and we should know that whatever service we are so graciously and mercifully offered by Krishna through His representatives, it is our fortune, it is our right. If we accepted with such a heart of gratitude and obedience, then we can make proper spiritual advancement. But if we see it materially then you will never see Krishna. We should try to see Krishna in our service. Do not try to see Krishna but serve Krishna in such a way that He will want to see you. That means we must learn to look for Krishna within our service.

Devotional service is the very energy of Srimati Radharani and therefore devotional service is nondifferent than Srimati Radharani. So, by seeing devotional service in a material way by complaining about the type of service that is given to us what we are doing is, we are complaining about how Sri Sri RadhaGopinath is appearing to me. When Krishna so mercifully appears, it is not polite to say, “Krishna, why are you appearing like this, I want to like this instead. I do not like you appearing like this. This is not a very nice way to appear”. That’s what you are doing, literarily. If you are asked to do something, even if you do not like it, you should understand that this is your service. This is Radharani’s appearance in your life, out of her mercy. No no Radharani, I do not like you like this. I like you like this instead. Of course, if we are in the enjoying mood then we are very particular about what we like to do. But if we are properly in the service attitude then we allow the master to be particular. We simply carry out the order. We simply follow the order. This is our great privilege. Devotional service is very rare privilege. If you examine the world, we live in there are billions of people in the human form of life and practically all of them are engaged in materialistic activities which are karma bandhan. Simply further entangling them in the source of Karma, the sources of miserable conditions of life. How few, how rare it is to find someone, who is engaged in those pure spiritual activities under the guidance of guru maharaja that are liberating them form the cycle of birth and death. To achieve devotional service is so rare. To have the right, to have the privilege of engaging in the devotional service is such a rare rare benediction that we should not be so proud that we are fussy about what type of service Radha and Krishna are giving to me. We should be grateful for whatever. Of course, often time new devotees are like this because they are so frustrated in the struggle of material existence, when they hear the philosophy then they see that oh, “I can wash a pot for Krishna”. If it is washing that pot with his life and soul because it is for Krishna. Because it is the first time in their life, they are doing something liberating them rather than keeping them in bondage.

Then what happens after we are devotees for some time, we are given so much devotional service which is so rare that we start to not understand, what we have. We start to think that the diamonds of devotional service are no better than glass and neglect them. If you have a rarest diamond but you do not take care of it, what happens then you lose it. There is an example of this.

If one has the most beautiful wonderful jewel that if you do not understand the value of that jewel and therefore, he does not properly be attentive to protect it and maintain. Eventually that jewel is either lost or stolen. Now what is the condition of that person? He is most unfortunate. Because he had the most beautiful thing or because he did not understand its value, he was careless and was taken away. When he wants to get that jewel back may be a long time, may be not in this life, may be not in many lives. So, you are given the jewel of bhakti of the right to serve Krishna, which is the only singular means of going back to godhead or liberating oneself in loving devotion to god. But sometimes we become so familiar, we just do not see the value of we have and we neglect it, and we are careless with it. And when Krishna sees we are neglected and careless eventually He will take it away and then we are the most unfortunate. So therefore, by hearing properly Srimad Bhagavatam we can understand the great fortunes we are given in devotional service. When we understand how karma bandhan activities are keeping everyone bound to the cycle of birth and death then how only through the process of engaging in devotional activities that we can free ourselves. We understand how important it is.

Srila Prabhupada told us that on the path of bhakti they are two sections. There is pancaratrika vidhi and there is Bhagavad Dharma. Pancaratrika vidhi means the service that we render to the deity of Lord and Bhagavad Dharma is the process of hearing and chanting the glories of the Lord. Now they both are essential. But Bhagavad Dharma must be very carefully understood and must be very very sincerely accepted, why? Because if we are not properly hearing Srimad Bhagavatam, then we begin to see our pancaratrika vidhi activities is something material, inevitably. We begin think about devotional service as just the job. Just like the karmi have their jobs. Just like the karmis work from 9 to 5. So, I also put my hours for the temple. I also put my hours for my guru. This is what happens if you do not properly hear but if we properly hear the message of godhead this mentality will never come about. It is because we are constantly reminded of the rare fortune what we are given that we have the right to serve Sri Hari. We are being given the most precious jewel of devotional service and how we care for it, how we prize it, how we will be grateful, so whatever little service we have the fortune of executing. This is very important. In fact, Srila Prabhupada told us that if you are not attentively and properly hearing and chanting, that means chanting the holy names according to prescribed number with attention and attentively hearing Bhagavata philosophy. If you are not doing both, he warned us what will happen. Not might happen but what will happen. He said, eventually you will look at RadhaVrindavanChandra or RadhaGopinath and you will understand all the service you have for them and will think why has my spiritual master left me such a burden? Rather than thinking my spiritual master is so kind such a wonderful benediction that he is giving me the right to serve RadhaGopinath and with the heart full of light and enthusiasm, we just gratefully engage in any little tiny morsel of service that we can find for RadhaGopinath. That life is what makes Krishna Consciousness beautiful that is the result of properly hearing the philosophy and chanting the holy names. But if we are not hearing and chanting regularly attentively then those same activities, you will simply see as burden I have to do this. Why my guru has left me today to have to do this? Why can’t, somebody else do it. Why me? Isn’t there anyone else?

Krishna reveals Himself according to how you approach Him. If you are thinking Krishna is not there, someone else can do this. Krishna sees, this is how he is looking at me. He is none different than devotional service. That service is god. That service is Radharani. If you are saying Krishna, isn’t there somebody else who could do this? It makes devotional service looks like something that very unwanted, very lowly. If you look at Krishna like that He will reciprocate accordingly. He will say, you do not want me. Then you live your life rascal. That is how Krishna would see. “I am appearing to you in this most beautiful and merciful way and you do not want me. You are complaining about me”. Then you go on. You simply be satisfied in your complaints. You will simply be satisfied in your own mental illusion. But if you look for satisfying Krishna through our service then you will be satisfied. This is what is meant by Yoga to control the mind. To control the mind by directing our mind in such a way that we understand, what is devotional service and we train our mind to be enthusiastic and happy simply for the opportunity to satisfy our guru, to satisfy Krishna

“karmaṇy evādhikāras te

mā phaleṣu kadācana

mā karma-phala-hetur bhūr

mā te saṅgo 'stv akarmaṇi”

Bhagavad-Gita 2.47

You have the right; you have the great fortune of engaging in your duty but never think that you are entitled to the fruits of your actions. If you see or you think that you are entitled to the fruits of your action then yes, you will be very fussy– and you will be very much particular about what type of service you like to do because you are attached to the fruits. But if you understand that Krishna is exclusive enjoyer of those fruits then does not matter what you do. It is being done for Krishna’s enjoyment. Is devotional service being done for your enjoyment or is being done for Krishna’s enjoyment? To that degree you think that it is being done for your enjoyment to that degree you cannot taste the sweetness of bhakti and to the degree you simply do it for Krishna’s enjoyment to that degree you can taste the sweetness of bhakti. We find that those devotees that are happiest and that are most enthusiastic are those devotees will do anything because it pleases Krishna, very simple. The devotees that are the most miserable, the most morose the least enthusiastic are those devotees who are very particular and choosy about what I like to do. The most surrendered you are the most blissful you become. We can see with our own eyes. But we are sometimes very foolish. We are thinking if I do this then I will be trapped and I will be miserable.

Prahalad Maharaja said, “If you want to be happy stop trying to be happy”. As long as you are trying to be happiness in your devotional service you can be in miserable. It’s a fact. As soon as you just stop trying to find happiness in your devotional service and if you think Krishna is happy. Krishna is happy when you simply do it, when you obey/ when you surrender then you will become filled with life enthusiasm and ecstasy. So, this consciousness has a completely opposite effect. When we try to find happiness, we find misery. And when we try to give happiness then we find happiness. This is the eternal law on the spiritual path. Krishna is the enjoyer of the fruits of our activities. We are not the enjoyer of our activities. Let us simply do our service to be enjoyed by Krishna and what does Krishna enjoy? He enjoys our surrender the quality of our surrender. He does not enjoy the results. Someone was asking recently, what if someone tells you to do something and you know that you can do something better if you do in your way. But your guru wants to you do in another way and you know you cannot do it is good that way. So, what is the answer?

Does Krishna care how you good do you something? Or does Krishna care about your surrender? Krishna does not need people doing things good for him. He could do it better Himself. He does not need you rascal. You do not think He could do it. He is simply giving you the opportunity to surrender by giving you that service. He wants your surrender. Your duty is you must do it the best you can that’s the quality of your surrender. But you must do it the best you can the way He wants it. Krishna is simply looking for that quality of devotion. How to satisfy Him and how to please Him? Because He is the enjoyer of the fruits of our action and the fruit that He enjoys the quality of the bhakti, the quality of surrender. Never consider yourself to be the cause of the results of your activities. This means that Krishna is the doer. However hard you try, whoever you are, your activity does not determine the result. It is in the higher powers.

Just like you read in Mahabharata about the great battle between Arjuna and Karna. What an incredible battle it was. Actually, Karna was of the two better fighters and he had very very special powerful weapons, the shakti, nagastra. These weapons could both killed Arjuna and he knew it. In that wonderful battle, ultimately Arjuna was victorious and Radheya knew that was not because of Arjuna’s skills and it was not because of Radheya’s lack of skills. Radheya understood it is my faith that I have to loose, no matter how good I fight. It does not matter what I do. I am going to loose this battle. He knew this toward the end. He saw that all of his efforts beyond his control. They were all being foiled by Krishna. If Krishna wants Arjuna to win, Arjuna is going to win. Does not matter how great you are. If Krishna wants Karna to loose, he is going to loose. Krishna is the cause of the results of our activities. The real wining or loosing is to surrender. The real winning and loosing is not to obtain the result. If you obtain the right result, never be proud. If Krishna who is giving you that result and if you obtain the wrong result, don’t lament because Krishna is giving you the wrong result. Actually, it is the right result. Success and failure are words for the gross materialist. A devotee never sees anything in terms of success or failure. If you are honestly trying to serve your spiritual master and Krishna that can only be success. There is no such thing as failure. In the material world people may say, “You are failure”. But we do not care for that. We do not care for them. If Krishna is satisfied, then we are success.

So never consider yourself to be the cause of the results of your activities. But then Krishna goes on very important end of the sloka because the tendency is if I am not the cause of your result of your activities then why should I do it? Krishna will do it any way. If Krishna wants me to collect money and I am not the cause of the result of collecting money, if Krishna is ultimately collecting the money then let Him collect, why should I do it? Krishna said, never be attached of not doing your duty. You must do your duty. That you must do it with detachment. This is what we are reading about in today’s verse. Performing activities with detachment.

Detachment means we are simply doing it for the pleasure of Krishna. We are simply doing it in an act of service. We are not the enjoyer nor we consider ourselves to be the cause of the result but we very very carefully and obediently execute our duty. To understand these simply principles is real detachment. Krishna says, “Never be attached to not doing, what you are doing”. “To not do your duty in attachment”. To do your duty in this proper spirit of consciousness is detachment and it is describing here how lord Krishna by His own example, he teaches us, Krishna creates, He maintains and annihilates the entire universe. But he is not attached to any of it. He is simply does it as the facility for the living beings. He is not attached. He is completely detached.

So, If Krishna is detached from creating, maintaining, and annihilating the entire cosmic manifestation, what is our position? What is the magnitude of what we are doing compare to that? We are doing it so insignificant and small and yet we become attached. So, therefore Krishna is showing us to His example how to live. To perform our duties without attachment. To perform our duties for the pleasure of Krishna and guru and the representative of His devotees. And simply by understanding this principle there will be no impediments in our spiritual progress. But it is very important in order to have this spirit that we must chant the holy names sincerely and we must here Srimad Bhagavatam everyday with a very very attentive heart. If we are not doing these two things it will be impossible for us to perform our service in this detached spirit. The spirit of bhakti. “śravaṇaṁ kīrtanaṁ viṣṇoḥ”. The process of hearing and chanting is the food which nourishes our heart and gives us the strength to engage in devotional service in this proper consciousness.

Q&A Transcription

Is there any questions?

QD:…………?

AM: Did not Arjuna try to win the war but ultimately it is not within your control. Krishna makes the outcome in a different way. You simply accept it. You have to try for success. If you do not try for success, then you are not a servant. You must try for success. Just in the trying you are successful. The result is nothing to do it whether you are success or failure. It may or may not but not necessarily. It is the effort that is the success. Krishna told Arjuna whether you win or loose it does not a matter. Just do it and fight for me. He said, “If you will win, you will gain the whole world. If you will loose, you will go back to heavens”. What is the difference? Just do it for me. That is success.

Any other questions?

Thank you very much.

Hare Krishna.

End of the transcription.
